jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Homer crashes his car in the opening sequence
jokes.punchline: His signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Homer's iconic catchphrase, perfectly integrated into character and show's DNA

jokes.text: Hey, I don't do The Jewish stuff on the air.
jokes.setup: Someone mentions Krusty's birthday and Jewish heritage
jokes.punchline: Krusty's shameful rejection of his roots
jokes.explanation: Sharp character moment showing Krusty's insecurity about his identity

jokes.text: Ix-nay on the ew-Jay. Roll the cartoon.
jokes.setup: Krusty wants to avoid Jewish references
jokes.punchline: Uses pig latin to say 'nix on the Jew' - ironically making it more obvious
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that makes the concealment more conspicuous - good ironic structure

jokes.text: It's so sad that Krusty is ashamed of his roots.
jokes.setup: Krusty just awkwardly avoided his Jewish identity
jokes.punchline: Marge's earnest, obvious observation
jokes.explanation: Marge's innocent directness contrasts with the awkward moment

jokes.text: Marge, it happened again.
jokes.setup: Marge makes an obvious observation
jokes.punchline: Homer's weary recognition of her stating the obvious
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer response - tired recognition of his wife's tendency to state the obvious

jokes.text: This is a rather lifeless outing.
jokes.setup: Watching a bad Itchy & Scratchy cartoon
jokes.punchline: Lisa's overly sophisticated critique of cartoon violence
jokes.explanation: Lisa's precocious vocabulary applied to lowbrow content is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Don't worry. They're building to something.
jokes.setup: Lisa critiques the cartoon's weakness
jokes.punchline: Bart's false optimism about obvious hack writing
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on lazy cartoon writing through Bart's misplaced faith

jokes.text: Ehh, I could pull a better cartoon out of my a-- [Chuckles] Hey! Whoa! Wasn't that great, kids?
jokes.setup: Krusty about to swear on children's television
jokes.punchline: Awkward self-correction and fake enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Perfect Krusty moment - unprofessional slip followed by desperate damage control

jokes.text: That's as bad as the tasteless 'Itchy and Sambo' cartoons of the late '30s.
jokes.setup: Critiquing the bad cartoon
jokes.punchline: Reference to racist cartoon history
jokes.explanation: Sharp historical reference that's both educational and satirical - sophisticated humor

jokes.text: Cartoons have writers? Ehh, sort of.
jokes.setup: Lisa mentions cartoon writers
jokes.punchline: Homer's dismissive 'sort of' - implying they're barely writers
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about animation writing quality delivered with Homer's casual dismissiveness

jokes.text: Bart, are you thinking what I'm thinking? Probably not.
jokes.setup: Lisa asks if Bart is thinking what she's thinking
jokes.punchline: Bart's immediate honest 'probably not'
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion of the cliché 'are you thinking what I'm thinking' with Bart's self-aware honesty

jokes.text: Lie in the snow and count to 60.
jokes.setup: Lisa wondering what Bart is thinking
jokes.punchline: Bart's random, pointless thought
jokes.explanation: Perfectly random Bart thought that confirms Lisa's suspicion - absurd and character-specific

jokes.text: 'Third notice'? 'Final notice'? 'Some guys are coming'?
jokes.setup: Reading increasingly urgent mail
jokes.punchline: The vague threat of 'some guys are coming'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from formal notices to ominous colloquial threat

jokes.text: Gee, that's odd. They didn't send one to you.
jokes.setup: Homer mentioning the high school reunion invitation
jokes.punchline: His oblivious observation that Marge didn't get one
jokes.explanation: Sets up Homer's secret reveal - his innocent observation creates dramatic irony

jokes.text: This is it, Homer. It's time to tell her the terrible secret from your past.
jokes.setup: Homer's internal monologue about revealing a dark secret
jokes.punchline: Creates expectation for something truly serious
jokes.explanation: Sets up expectation for major revelation - works as setup for following jokes

jokes.text: Marge, I ate those fancy soaps you bought for the bathroom.
jokes.setup: The 'terrible secret' buildup
jokes.punchline: Mundane confession about eating soap
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection from 'terrible secret' to absurd but character-appropriate confession

jokes.text: No, the other secret.
jokes.setup: Wrong secret revealed
jokes.punchline: There's another, presumably worse secret
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that implies Homer has multiple shameful secrets

jokes.text: Well, that still doesn't explain why you ate my soap. Wait. Maybe it does.
jokes.setup: Two separate confessions
jokes.punchline: Marge connects lack of education to soap consumption
jokes.explanation: Clever callback that logically connects Homer's two shameful admissions

jokes.text: Marge, ix-nay on the uclear-nay echnician-tay.
jokes.setup: Marge mentions he's a nuclear technician without graduating
jokes.punchline: Homer uses pig latin like Krusty did, but incorrectly
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to Krusty's pig latin with Homer's characteristic bungling of it

jokes.text: What did you say? I don't know. I flunked Latin too.
jokes.setup: Homer's mangled pig latin
jokes.punchline: He admits he failed Latin, explaining his incompetence
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up explaining Homer's pig latin failure with another academic failure

jokes.text: opens a box of flesh-eating ants... and the rest writes itself.
jokes.setup: Describing violent cartoon scenario
jokes.punchline: 'The rest writes itself' - casual attitude toward extreme violence
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about cartoon violence with meta-commentary on lazy writing

jokes.text: All right, then to decide it I propose a race around the world. Meet me at Leicester Square at noon tomorrow. The queen herself shall drop the checkered flag.
jokes.setup: Arguing over whose name goes first
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously elaborate solution to trivial problem
jokes.explanation: Hilariously overwrought proposal that contrasts with Bart's simple solution

jokes.text: Look, there's only one reasonable way to settle this-- rock, paper, scissors.
jokes.setup: Lisa's elaborate world race proposal
jokes.punchline: Bart's pragmatic, childish solution
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast between Lisa's overthinking and Bart's simple practicality

jokes.text: Poor, predictable Bart. Always takes rock. Good old rock. Nothing beats that.
jokes.setup: Both thinking they know the other's strategy
jokes.punchline: Both wrong about their own superiority
jokes.explanation: Brilliant simultaneous internal monologues showing both characters' flawed confidence

jokes.text: If I puked in a fountain pen and mailed it to the monkey house, I'd get better scripts.
jokes.setup: Criticizing bad writing
jokes.punchline: Elaborately disgusting metaphor for script quality
jokes.explanation: Incredibly creative and disgusting insult that's both memorable and character-appropriate

jokes.text: Oh, at Harvard they taught you? Hit the streets, egghead! You should've majored in not getting fired.
jokes.setup: Writer mentions Harvard education
jokes.punchline: Fake college major insult
jokes.explanation: Sharp put-down that plays on college major format for comedic effect

jokes.text: You, sir, have the boorish manners of a Yalie!
jokes.setup: Being insulted about Harvard
jokes.punchline: Retorts with Yale insult - ivy league rivalry
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific insult playing on Harvard-Yale rivalry and pretentious language

jokes.text: He let those guys use his checkbook for a whole year.
jokes.setup: Bart explaining why Grampa is a good choice as fake writer
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of Grampa being scammed
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about elderly exploitation presented matter-of-factly by Bart

jokes.text: Well, whenever I'm confused I just check my underwear. It holds the answer to all the important questions.
jokes.setup: Being asked his first name
jokes.punchline: Checks underwear for life's answers
jokes.explanation: Perfect Grampa absurdity - treats underwear as oracle for all life decisions

jokes.text: Grampa, how'd you take off your underwear without taking off your pants? I don't know!
jokes.setup: Grampa checking underwear while fully dressed
jokes.punchline: Logical impossibility that even Grampa can't explain
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual impossibility that even the perpetrator can't explain

jokes.text: That's right. I did the Iggy.
jokes.setup: Asked about writing Itchy and Scratchy
jokes.punchline: Calls it 'the Iggy' - completely wrong name
jokes.explanation: Typical Grampa confusion getting names wrong in endearing way

jokes.text: Time to go to the reunion. It'll be great to see the old gang again-- Potsie, Ralph Malph, the Fonz.
jokes.setup: Going to his high school reunion
jokes.punchline: Lists Happy Days characters as his classmates
jokes.explanation: Homer's confusion of TV with reality - perfectly in character

jokes.text: That was Happy Days. No, they weren't all happy days. Like the time Pinky Tuscadero crashed her motorcycle. Or the night I lost all my money to those card sharks... and my dad Tom Bosley had to get it back.
jokes.setup: Marge corrects him about Happy Days
jokes.punchline: Homer continues the delusion, casting Tom Bosley as his father
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of Homer's delusion with specific episode references and casting himself in the show

jokes.text: Look, there's Bobby Mindich, the class clown. I... am not a crook! [Laughs] Eh? Richard Nixon. I know!
jokes.setup: Identifying the class clown
jokes.punchline: His 'comedy' is just Nixon impressions
jokes.explanation: Weak joke about dated impressions being the height of comedy in the past

jokes.text: Sorry, sir. We're not letting vagrants sleep in the gym tonight. But we will be putting some scraps by the back door.
jokes.setup: Homer approaching the principal at the reunion
jokes.punchline: Principal mistakes Homer for a homeless person
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Homer's humiliation - the principal's assumption about his appearance

jokes.text: Hello, Marge. Have you heard? I'm stinking rich. Jealous?
jokes.setup: Meeting ex-boyfriend at reunion
jokes.punchline: Immediately brags about wealth and asks if she's jealous
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Artie's tactless bragging at reunion

jokes.text: I'll bet you'd trade it all for one night with my wife. I would.
jokes.setup: Responding to Artie's bragging
jokes.punchline: Homer would trade his life for sex with his own wife
jokes.explanation: Clever self-deprecating joke about Homer's own marriage situation

jokes.text: Hey, Man, it's Dave! Open up, Man! Dave's not here, Man. The guy's so high, he doesn't even know that's Dave!
jokes.setup: Cheech and Chong reference at reunion
jokes.punchline: Explaining the reference ruins it
jokes.explanation: Weak reference humor that's both dated and poorly executed

jokes.text: How'd you do it, Homer? I discovered a meal between breakfast and brunch.
jokes.setup: Asked how he gained the most weight
jokes.punchline: Homer invented a fourth morning meal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic - his approach to overeating is systematic and innovative

jokes.text: It hasn't been easy staying in my rut.
jokes.setup: Winning multiple awards for negative achievements
jokes.punchline: Takes pride in his lack of progress
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - finding pride in stagnation with sincere delivery

jokes.text: This is Howard Co-smell. Homer Simpson is a boob. What do you think, J.J.? Dy-no-mite!
jokes.setup: TV personalities commenting on Homer's humiliation
jokes.punchline: Outdated celebrity impressions delivering harsh judgment
jokes.explanation: Dated celebrity impressions - captures the era's TV culture but not particularly strong humor

jokes.text: Barney, where's your cummerbund? It fell in the toilet.
jokes.setup: Formal event attire question
jokes.punchline: Barney's matter-of-fact gross explanation
jokes.explanation: Typical Barney grossness delivered with characteristic casual tone

jokes.text: You're a comedy writer? My God, you're so old. I want my check!
jokes.setup: Being told he's a writer
jokes.punchline: Fixates on age and money instead
jokes.explanation: Grampa's scattered attention span and priorities perfectly displayed

jokes.text: His name is Abraham Simpson... and he's got something you couldn't get at your fancy schools-- life experience. I spent 40 years as a night watchman at a cranberry silo.
jokes.setup: Praising Grampa's 'life experience'
jokes.punchline: The experience is incredibly mundane
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of 'life experience' rhetoric with hilariously specific boring job

jokes.text: Dad, whatever you say... you know you'll always have my love and respect. I never graduated from high school. [Laughing] [Choking]
jokes.setup: Lisa's touching speech about unconditional love
jokes.punchline: Bart's laughter at Homer's confession gets him choked
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Lisa's sincerity and Bart's mockery, with physical payback

jokes.text: They pay me $800 a week to tell a cat and mouse what to do!
jokes.setup: Describing his important job
jokes.punchline: Reduces cartoon writing to giving orders to animals
jokes.explanation: Grampa's literal interpretation of cartoon writing is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: All right. But if he starts to wig out, try to lure him into the cellar.
jokes.setup: Kids wanting to talk to Grampa alone
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual instructions for dealing with Grampa's episodes
jokes.explanation: Dark family humor - Homer's matter-of-fact approach to elderly care

jokes.text: I dreamt I was the queen of the Old West. I kept a six-shooter in my garter, I did.
jokes.setup: Being woken from a nice dream
jokes.punchline: Grampa's bizarrely specific cross-dressing cowboy fantasy
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully bizarre and specific dream that's perfectly Grampa-esque

jokes.text: Sometimes to save money... our animators will reuse the same backgrounds over and over and over again.
jokes.setup: Explaining cost-cutting measures
jokes.punchline: Meta-joke about animation shortcuts (likely accompanied by repetitive backgrounds)
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-commentary on animation industry practices, likely with visual demonstration

jokes.text: My, uh, wife recently passed away. I thought teaching might ease my loneliness. Will this be on the test? No! Oh!
jokes.setup: Teacher sharing personal tragedy to connect with students
jokes.punchline: Student's immediate selfish concern about test relevance
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor - student's complete lack of empathy is both realistic and horrible

jokes.text: The bright blue flame indicates this was a particularly sweet doughnut.
jokes.setup: Science class demonstration
jokes.punchline: Treating burning donut as legitimate scientific experiment
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor treating junk food as scientific specimen

jokes.text: From now on, the fate of the entire company rests on that delightful coconut of yours.
jokes.setup: Putting all pressure on Grampa
jokes.punchline: Calling his head a 'delightful coconut'
jokes.explanation: Amusing metaphor for Grampa's bald head while revealing business desperation

jokes.text: All right, brain. You don't like me, and I don't like you... but let's just do this and I can get back to killing you with beer.
jokes.setup: Homer needs to think for his test
jokes.punchline: Negotiates with his brain like a separate entity, promises to destroy it with alcohol
jokes.explanation: One of the best Homer quotes ever - personifies his brain as adversary while admitting his self-destructive habits

jokes.text: It's a deal!
jokes.setup: Homer's negotiation with his brain
jokes.punchline: Brain eagerly agrees to the deal
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up showing even Homer's brain wants to escape from Homer

jokes.text: Well, here we are. The star of The Blue Lagoon and me, the blue-haired goon!
jokes.setup: Presenting with Brooke Shields
jokes.punchline: Krusty's self-deprecating rhyme about his appearance
jokes.explanation: Decent self-deprecating joke but fairly predictable rhyming structure

jokes.text: What the-- That's terrible! First of all, my hair is green, not blue. I got nothin' to work with here. Nothin'!
jokes.setup: His scripted joke falls flat
jokes.punchline: Krusty's professional frustration and correction about his hair color
jokes.explanation: Good meta-moment about bad writing and performer frustration with material

jokes.text: Cross my fingers for me. [Snap] That's gonna hurt come winter.
jokes.setup: Asking for luck at awards show
jokes.punchline: Literally breaks his fingers crossing them, matter-of-fact about future pain
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with Grampa's characteristic old-age frailty and deadpan delivery

jokes.text: That was the first time I ever saw Itchy and Scratchy... and I didn't like it one bit! It was disgusting and violent! I think all you people are despicable! For shame!
jokes.setup: Winning award for writing the cartoon
jokes.punchline: Condemns the show he supposedly wrote
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ironic moment where the 'creator' condemns his own work, revealing the fraud

jokes.text: I'll never watch an awards show again. Unless that delightful Billy Crystal's involved.
jokes.setup: Bart swearing off awards shows after the debacle
jokes.punchline: His immediate exception for a specific entertainer
jokes.explanation: Good character inconsistency joke - Bart's immediate exception undermines his grand statement

jokes.text: At our next high school reunion, I'll have nothing to be ashamed of. Simpson. Is that a plunger stuck on your head? D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer's confidence about having his diploma
jokes.punchline: Immediately gets embarrassed by plunger on head
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Homer's newfound confidence with immediate physical humiliation

jokes.text: Simpson. Is that a plunger stuck on your head? D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer returning to face the principal after graduation
jokes.punchline: He has a plunger stuck on his head, ending with his catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag that fits Homer's hapless character - solid ending punctuation

jokes.text: We're not going to church today. [Gasps] What? You give me one good reason! It's Saturday! Okilly-dokilly-do!
jokes.setup: Marge's shock at skipping church
jokes.punchline: Simple explanation it's Saturday, Ned's characteristic response
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective joke about religious confusion with Ned's catchphrase
